jobs.title: Ticket 01: Flutter Project Setup + Dependencies
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 1 — Foundation (MVP)
**Blocked by:** None
**Blocks:** #02, #03, #04, #05

---

## Summary

Initialize the Flutter project, install all dependencies, and configure platform permissions for microphone and speech recognition on iOS and Android.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Create Flutter project

```bash
flutter create --org com.studybuddy --project-name studybuddy --platforms ios,android .
```

### 2. Add runtime dependencies

| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `flutter_riverpod` | State management |
| `riverpod_annotation` | Riverpod annotations |
| `flutter_tts` | Text-to-speech (Korean + English) |
| `speech_to_text` | Speech recognition (Korean) |
| `drift` | Local SQLite database (type-safe) |
| `sqlite3_flutter_libs` | SQLite native bindings |
| `path_provider` | App documents directory for DB file |
| `dio` | HTTP client for backend API |
| `go_router` | Declarative routing |
| `freezed_annotation` | Immutable model annotations |
| `json_annotation` | JSON serialization annotations |
| `uuid` | Generate unique IDs |
| `collection` | Collection utilities (PriorityQueue) |

### 3. Add dev dependencies

| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `build_runner` | Code generation runner |
| `freezed` | Generates freezed model code |
| `json_serializable` | Generates JSON serialization code |
| `drift_dev` | Generates Drift database code |

> **Note:** `riverpod_generator` conflicts with `drift_dev` on the `analyzer` package. Skip it — use Riverpod providers manually instead.

### 4. Configure iOS permissions

In `ios/Runner/Info.plist`, add inside `<dict>`:

```xml
<key>NSMicrophoneUsageDescription</key>
<string>StudyBuddy needs microphone access to listen to your Korean pronunciation during drill sessions.</string>
<key>NSSpeechRecognitionUsageDescription</key>
<string>StudyBuddy uses speech recognition to evaluate your Korean pronunciation.</string>
```

### 5. Configure Android permissions

In `android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`, add before `<application>`:

```xml
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.RECORD_AUDIO"/>
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.INTERNET"/>
```

### 6. Create directory structure

```
lib/
├── main.dart
├── app.dart
├── core/
│   ├── theme.dart
│   └── router.dart
├── data/
│   ├── database/
│   │   └── app_database.dart
│   └── repositories/
│       ├── lesson_repository.dart
│       └── progress_repository.dart
├── domain/
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── lesson_item.dart
│   │   ├── user_progress.dart
│   │   ├── drill_item.dart
│   │   └── drill_session.dart
│   └── services/
│       ├── drill_engine.dart
│       ├── drill_evaluator.dart
│       ├── tts_service.dart
│       └── stt_service.dart
└── features/
    ├── home/
    │   └── home_screen.dart
    ├── drill/
    │   ├── drill_screen.dart
    │   ├── drill_result_screen.dart
    │   └── widgets/
    │       ├── waveform_indicator.dart
    │       ├── score_display.dart
    │       └── hint_card.dart
    ├── lessons/
    │   └── lesson_list_screen.dart
    └── progress/
        └── progress_screen.dart
```

### 7. Verify

- `flutter pub get` succeeds with no conflicts
- `flutter analyze` passes
- App builds and runs on iOS simulator showing default counter app

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Flutter project created with correct org and platforms
- [ ] All dependencies resolve without conflicts
- [ ] iOS Info.plist has microphone + speech recognition permission strings
- [ ] Android manifest has RECORD_AUDIO + INTERNET permissions
- [ ] Directory structure created
- [ ] App builds on iOS simulator

jobs.title: Ticket 02: Data Models + Drift Database Schema
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 1 — Foundation (MVP)
**Blocked by:** #01
**Blocks:** #06, #09

---

## Summary

Define all domain models using freezed and the local SQLite database schema using Drift. This establishes the data layer that everything else builds on.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Freezed models

#### `lib/domain/models/lesson_item.dart`

```dart
enum LessonItemType { vocabulary, phrase, grammarPattern, dialogTurn }

@freezed
class LessonItem {
  id: String (UUID)
  korean: String            // e.g. "감사합니다"
  romanization: String      // e.g. "gamsahamnida"
  english: String           // e.g. "thank you (formal)"
  type: LessonItemType
  difficulty: int (1-5)
  contextNote: String       // extra context from tutor session
  tags: List<String>        // e.g. ["formal", "greeting"]
  sessionId: String?        // FK to tutor session (null for seed data)
}
```

#### `lib/domain/models/user_progress.dart`

```dart
@freezed
class UserProgress {
  itemId: String            // FK to LessonItem
  easeFactor: double        // SM-2 ease factor, default 2.5
  intervalSeconds: int      // current SRS interval
  nextReviewAt: DateTime    // when this item is next due
  repetitionCount: int      // times successfully recalled
  lastScore: double         // 0.0-1.0 from most recent drill
  lastReviewedAt: DateTime? // null if never reviewed
}
```

#### `lib/domain/models/drill_session.dart`

```dart
@freezed
class DrillSession {
  id: String (UUID)
  startedAt: DateTime
  endedAt: DateTime?
  itemsDrilled: int
  averageScore: double
}
```

#### `lib/domain/models/drill_item.dart`

Non-freezed mutable class used at runtime during drill sessions:

```dart
enum DrillType { promptedRecall, dialogSimulation }
enum DrillResult { pass, partial, fail }

class DrillItem {
  final LessonItem lessonItem
  final UserProgress progress
  final DrillType drillType
  final String promptEnglish      // "How do you say 'thank you'?"
  final String expectedKorean     // "감사합니다"
  final List<String> hints        // progressive hints

  // Mutable drill state
  int currentHintIndex = 0
  double? score
  String? userResponse
  DrillResult? result
}

class ScheduledDrill implements Comparable<ScheduledDrill> {
  final DrillItem item
  final Duration scheduledAt      // session-clock time to present
  final bool isIntroduction       // first time seeing this item
}
```

### 2. Drift database tables

#### `lib/data/database/app_database.dart`

Define three Drift tables:

**LessonItems table:**
- `id` TEXT PK
- `korean` TEXT
- `romanization` TEXT
- `english` TEXT
- `type` TEXT (enum as string)
- `difficulty` INTEGER default 1
- `contextNote` TEXT default ''
- `tags` TEXT default '[]' (JSON array stored as string)
- `sessionId` TEXT nullable
- `createdAt` DATETIME default currentDateAndTime

**UserProgressTable:**
- `itemId` TEXT PK, FK → LessonItems.id
- `easeFactor` REAL default 2.5
- `intervalSeconds` INTEGER default 0
- `nextReviewAt` DATETIME
- `repetitionCount` INTEGER default 0
- `lastScore` REAL default 0.0
- `lastReviewedAt` DATETIME nullable

**DrillSessions table:**
- `id` TEXT PK
- `startedAt` DATETIME
- `endedAt` DATETIME nullable
- `itemsDrilled` INTEGER default 0
- `averageScore` REAL default 0.0

### 3. Database class with queries

```dart
@DriftDatabase(tables: [LessonItems, UserProgressTable, DrillSessions])
class AppDatabase extends _$AppDatabase {
  // schemaVersion = 1

  // Lesson item CRUD
  getAllLessonItems() → Future<List<LessonItem>>
  insertLessonItem(companion) → Future<void>
  insertAllLessonItems(companions) → Future<void> // batch insert

  // Progress queries
  getAllProgress() → Future<List<UserProgressTableData>>
  getProgressForItem(itemId) → Future<UserProgressTableData?>
  getDueItems() → Future<List<UserProgressTableData>>  // nextReviewAt <= now, sorted ASC
  upsertProgress(companion) → Future<void>

  // Drill session CRUD
  insertDrillSession(companion) → Future<void>
  updateDrillSession(companion) → Future<void>
  getRecentSessions(limit: 10) → Future<List<DrillSession>>

  // Combined queries
  getDueItemsWithContent() → Future<List<(LessonItem, UserProgressTableData)>>
      // JOIN lesson_items ON user_progress WHERE nextReviewAt <= now
  getTotalItemCount() → Future<int>
  getDueItemCount() → Future<int>
}
```

Database file opened via `LazyDatabase` using `path_provider` to get app documents directory, stored as `studybuddy.sqlite`.

### 4. Repository layer

#### `lib/data/repositories/lesson_repository.dart`

Thin wrapper over AppDatabase that converts between Drift row types and domain models:

```dart
class LessonRepository {
  final AppDatabase db;

  Future<List<LessonItem>> getAllItems()
  Future<void> addItem(LessonItem item)
  Future<void> addItems(List<LessonItem> items)
}
```

#### `lib/data/repositories/progress_repository.dart`

```dart
class ProgressRepository {
  final AppDatabase db;

  Future<List<UserProgress>> getDueItems()
  Future<void> updateProgress(UserProgress progress)
  Future<int> getDueCount()
  Future<int> getTotalCount()
}
```

### 5. Riverpod providers

```dart
// Database singleton
final databaseProvider = Provider<AppDatabase>((ref) => AppDatabase());

// Repositories
final lessonRepositoryProvider = Provider((ref) =>
    LessonRepository(ref.watch(databaseProvider)));
final progressRepositoryProvider = Provider((ref) =>
    ProgressRepository(ref.watch(databaseProvider)));

// Reactive queries
final dueItemCountProvider = FutureProvider<int>((ref) =>
    ref.watch(progressRepositoryProvider).getDueCount());
final totalItemCountProvider = FutureProvider<int>((ref) =>
    ref.watch(progressRepositoryProvider).getTotalCount());
```

### 6. Run code generation

```bash
dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs
```

This generates:
- `*.freezed.dart` for all freezed models
- `*.g.dart` for JSON serialization
- `app_database.g.dart` for Drift

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All freezed models compile and generate correctly
- [ ] Drift database creates tables on first launch
- [ ] Can insert a LessonItem and read it back
- [ ] Can insert UserProgress and query due items
- [ ] getDueItemsWithContent() JOIN works correctly
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 03: TTS Service — Korean + English Text-to-Speech
Ticket 04: STT Service — Korean Speech Recognition
Ticket 05: Korean Scoring Engine — Jamo Edit Distance
Ticket 06: Drill Engine — State Machine + Pimsleur Interval Scheduling
Ticket 07: Drill Screen UI — Voice-First Minimal Interface
Ticket 08: Home Screen + Navigation
Ticket 09: Seed Database with Korean Content
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 1 — Foundation (MVP)
**Blocked by:** #02
**Blocks:** None (but needed for end-to-end testing of all Phase 1 tickets)

---

## Summary

Create ~50 hand-curated Korean vocabulary and phrases across 3 difficulty levels. Seed the database on first app launch so the app has content to drill immediately without needing the backend ingestion pipeline.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Seed data file

#### `lib/data/seed_data.dart`

Organize items by topic and difficulty. Each item needs: korean, romanization, english, type, difficulty, tags.

**Difficulty 1 — Beginner (20 items):**

Greetings & basics:
```
안녕하세요        annyeonghaseyo      hello (formal)             [greeting, formal]
안녕              annyeong            hello (casual)             [greeting, casual]
감사합니다        gamsahamnida        thank you (formal)         [greeting, formal]
고마워요          gomawoyo            thank you (casual)         [greeting, casual]
네               ne                  yes                        [basic]
아니요            aniyo               no                         [basic]
죄송합니다        joesonghamnida      I'm sorry (formal)         [greeting, formal]
괜찮아요          gwaenchanayo        it's okay                  [basic]
```

Numbers & counting:
```
하나              hana                one (native)               [number]
둘               dul                 two (native)               [number]
셋               set                 three (native)             [number]
넷               net                 four (native)              [number]
다섯              daseot              five (native)              [number]
```

Common nouns:
```
물               mul                 water                      [noun, restaurant]
밥               bap                 rice / meal                [noun, food]
사람              saram               person                     [noun]
이름              ireum               name                       [noun]
```

Essential phrases:
```
이거 주세요       igeo juseyo         this one please            [phrase, restaurant]
얼마예요?         eolmayeyo           how much is it?            [phrase, shopping]
어디예요?         eodiyeyo            where is it?               [phrase, direction]
```

**Difficulty 2 — Elementary (15 items):**

Verbs & actions:
```
먹다              meokda              to eat                     [verb]
마시다            masida              to drink                   [verb]
가다              gada                to go                      [verb]
오다              oda                 to come                    [verb]
하다              hada                to do                      [verb]
```

Useful phrases:
```
저는 학생이에요    jeoneun haksaengieyo    I am a student         [phrase, introduction]
한국어를 공부해요  hangugeo-reul gongbuhaeyo I study Korean       [phrase]
이해했어요        ihaehae-sseoyo      I understood               [phrase]
다시 말해 주세요   dasi malhae juseyo  please say it again        [phrase, classroom]
천천히 말해 주세요 cheoncheonhi malhae juseyo please speak slowly [phrase, classroom]
```

Food & restaurant:
```
비빔밥            bibimbap            bibimbap                   [noun, food]
김치              gimchi              kimchi                     [noun, food]
맛있어요          masisseoyo          it's delicious             [phrase, food]
뭐 드시겠어요?    mwo deusigesseyo    what would you like?       [phrase, restaurant, formal]
계산서 주세요     gyesanseo juseyo    check please               [phrase, restaurant]
```

**Difficulty 3 — Intermediate (15 items):**

Grammar patterns:
```
먹고 싶어요       meokgo sipeoyo      I want to eat              [grammar, V-고 싶다]
갈 수 있어요      gal su isseoyo      I can go                   [grammar, V-(으)ㄹ 수 있다]
먹어 봤어요       meogeo bwasseoyo    I've tried eating          [grammar, V-아/어 보다]
공부하고 있어요   gongbuhago isseoyo  I am studying              [grammar, V-고 있다]
```

Longer phrases:
```
어제 친구를 만났어요   eoje chingureul mannasseoyo  I met a friend yesterday  [phrase, past tense]
내일 뭐 할 거예요?    naeil mwo hal geoyeyo       what will you do tomorrow? [phrase, future]
지하철역이 어디에 있어요? jihacheolyeogi eodie isseoyo where is the subway station? [phrase, direction]
택시를 타고 싶어요    taeksireul tago sipeoyo      I want to take a taxi     [phrase, transport]
```

Conversation:
```
한국에 얼마나 있었어요? hanguge eolmana isseosseoyo how long were you in Korea? [phrase, conversation]
한국 음식을 좋아해요   hanguk eumsigeul joahaeyo    I like Korean food        [phrase, food]
주말에 뭐 했어요?     jumale mwo haesseoyo         what did you do on the weekend? [phrase, conversation]
같이 가요            gachi gayo                   let's go together         [phrase, casual]
오래간만이에요        oraegan-manieyo              long time no see          [greeting, formal]
날씨가 좋아요        nalssiga joayo               the weather is nice       [phrase, conversation]
도와주세요           dowajuseyo                   please help me            [phrase]
```

### 2. Seed function

```dart
class DatabaseSeeder {
  final AppDatabase db;

  Future<void> seedIfEmpty() async {
    final count = await db.getTotalItemCount();
    if (count > 0) return; // already seeded

    final items = _seedItems();
    await db.insertAllLessonItems(items);

    // Initialize progress for all items — all due immediately
    for (final item in items) {
      await db.upsertProgress(UserProgressTableCompanion(
        itemId: Value(item.id.value),
        nextReviewAt: Value(DateTime.now()),
        easeFactor: const Value(2.5),
        intervalSeconds: const Value(0),
        repetitionCount: const Value(0),
        lastScore: const Value(0.0),
      ));
    }
  }

  List<LessonItemsCompanion> _seedItems() {
    return [
      _item('안녕하세요', 'annyeonghaseyo', 'hello (formal)', 1, ['greeting', 'formal']),
      // ... all items from above
    ];
  }

  LessonItemsCompanion _item(
    String korean, String romanization, String english,
    int difficulty, List<String> tags,
  ) {
    return LessonItemsCompanion(
      id: Value(const Uuid().v4()),
      korean: Value(korean),
      romanization: Value(romanization),
      english: Value(english),
      type: Value('vocabulary'),
      difficulty: Value(difficulty),
      tags: Value(jsonEncode(tags)),
    );
  }
}
```

### 3. Trigger seeding on app start

In `main.dart` or via a Riverpod provider that runs on startup:

```dart
final seedProvider = FutureProvider<void>((ref) async {
  final db = ref.watch(databaseProvider);
  await DatabaseSeeder(db).seedIfEmpty();
});
```

The home screen should watch `seedProvider` and show a loading indicator until seeding completes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 50 items across 3 difficulty levels
- [ ] Items cover: greetings, numbers, nouns, verbs, phrases, grammar patterns
- [ ] All items have correct romanization
- [ ] Tags accurately categorize each item
- [ ] Seeding runs only on first launch (idempotent)
- [ ] All items have corresponding user_progress rows with nextReviewAt = now
- [ ] App shows items in drill after seeding

jobs.title: Ticket 10: Backend Claude API Proxy
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 2 — Intelligence
**Blocked by:** #08 (Phase 1 complete)
**Blocks:** #11, #12, #15

---

## Summary

Build a lightweight FastAPI backend that proxies Claude API calls from the mobile app. This keeps the Anthropic API key server-side, enables rate limiting, request logging, and response caching. This is the foundation the ingestion pipeline (Phase 3) will extend.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Project setup

Create a `backend/` directory at the project root:

```
backend/
├── pyproject.toml          # or requirements.txt
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py             # FastAPI app
│   ├── config.py           # Settings (env vars)
│   ├── auth.py             # API key auth middleware
│   ├── routers/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   └── drill.py        # drill planning + evaluation endpoints
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   └── claude_service.py  # Claude API client wrapper
│   └── models/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       └── drill.py        # Pydantic request/response models
└── Dockerfile
```

### 2. Dependencies

```
fastapi
uvicorn
anthropic           # Anthropic Python SDK
pydantic
python-dotenv
```

### 3. Configuration

#### `app/config.py`

```python
from pydantic_settings import BaseSettings

class Settings(BaseSettings):
    anthropic_api_key: str
    app_api_key: str        # simple key the mobile app sends
    claude_model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-6-20250514"
    max_requests_per_minute: int = 20

    class Config:
        env_file = ".env"
```

### 4. Auth middleware

#### `app/auth.py`

Simple API key auth — the Flutter app sends `X-API-Key` header, backend validates it.

```python
from fastapi import Security, HTTPException
from fastapi.security import APIKeyHeader

api_key_header = APIKeyHeader(name="X-API-Key")

async def verify_api_key(api_key: str = Security(api_key_header)):
    if api_key != settings.app_api_key:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Invalid API key")
```

For MVP this is a single shared key. Phase 5 replaces this with Firebase Auth.

### 5. Claude service

#### `app/services/claude_service.py`

```python
import anthropic

class ClaudeService:
    def __init__(self, api_key: str, model: str):
        self.client = anthropic.Anthropic(api_key=api_key)
        self.model = model

    async def generate_drill_plan(self, items_json: list[dict], progress_json: list[dict]) -> dict:
        """Generate a Pimsleur-style drill session plan."""
        response = self.client.messages.create(
            model=self.model,
            max_tokens=4096,
            system="You are a Pimsleur-style Korean language drill instructor...",
            messages=[{
                "role": "user",
                "content": f"Here are the items...\n{json.dumps(items_json)}\n\nProgress:\n{json.dumps(progress_json)}\n\nGenerate a drill session plan as JSON..."
            }],
        )
        # Parse JSON from response
        return json.loads(response.content[0].text)

    async def evaluate_response(self, expected: str, actual: str, edit_distance_score: float) -> dict:
        """Evaluate an ambiguous Korean response."""
        response = self.client.messages.create(
            model=self.model,
            max_tokens=512,
            system="You are evaluating a Korean language learner's spoken response...",
            messages=[{
                "role": "user",
                "content": f"Expected Korean: {expected}\nUser said (via STT): {actual}\nMatch score: {edit_distance_score}\n\nRespond as JSON..."
            }],
        )
        return json.loads(response.content[0].text)
```

### 6. API endpoints

#### `app/routers/drill.py`

**POST `/api/drill/plan`**
- Request: `{ items: [...], progress: [...] }`
- Response: `{ session_items: [...] }` — Claude-generated session plan
- Used by the app at the start of each drill session

**POST `/api/drill/evaluate`**
- Request: `{ expected: "감사합니다", actual: "감사함니다", edit_distance_score: 0.85 }`
- Response: `{ correct: true, score: 0.9, feedback_english: "...", feedback_korean: "..." }`
- Used by the app when local scoring is ambiguous (0.4-0.8)

```python
@router.post("/api/drill/plan")
async def create_drill_plan(
    request: DrillPlanRequest,
    _: str = Depends(verify_api_key),
):
    plan = await claude_service.generate_drill_plan(
        items_json=request.items,
        progress_json=request.progress,
    )
    return plan

@router.post("/api/drill/evaluate")
async def evaluate_response(
    request: EvaluateRequest,
    _: str = Depends(verify_api_key),
):
    result = await claude_service.evaluate_response(
        expected=request.expected,
        actual=request.actual,
        edit_distance_score=request.edit_distance_score,
    )
    return result
```

### 7. Pydantic models

#### `app/models/drill.py`

```python
class LessonItemDTO(BaseModel):
    id: str
    korean: str
    romanization: str
    english: str
    type: str
    difficulty: int
    tags: list[str]

class ProgressDTO(BaseModel):
    item_id: str
    repetition_count: int
    last_score: float
    ease_factor: float

class DrillPlanRequest(BaseModel):
    items: list[LessonItemDTO]
    progress: list[ProgressDTO]

class EvaluateRequest(BaseModel):
    expected: str
    actual: str
    edit_distance_score: float

class EvaluateResponse(BaseModel):
    correct: bool
    score: float
    feedback_english: str
    feedback_korean: str
```

### 8. Flutter client update

#### `lib/data/api/claude_client.dart`

```dart
class ClaudeClient {
  final Dio _dio;

  ClaudeClient({required String baseUrl, required String apiKey})
      : _dio = Dio(BaseOptions(
          baseUrl: baseUrl,
          headers: {'X-API-Key': apiKey},
        ));

  Future<DrillPlan> generateDrillPlan({
    required List<LessonItem> items,
    required List<UserProgress> progress,
  }) async {
    final response = await _dio.post('/api/drill/plan', data: {
      'items': items.map((i) => i.toJson()).toList(),
      'progress': progress.map((p) => p.toJson()).toList(),
    });
    return DrillPlan.fromJson(response.data);
  }

  Future<ClaudeEvaluation> evaluateResponse({
    required String expected,
    required String actual,
    required double editDistanceScore,
  }) async {
    final response = await _dio.post('/api/drill/evaluate', data: {
      'expected': expected,
      'actual': actual,
      'edit_distance_score': editDistanceScore,
    });
    return ClaudeEvaluation.fromJson(response.data);
  }
}
```

### 9. Deployment

For MVP: run locally or deploy to a simple cloud service (Railway, Fly.io, or a small EC2 instance). Docker setup included for portability.

```dockerfile
FROM python:3.12-slim
WORKDIR /app
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install -r requirements.txt
COPY app/ app/
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] FastAPI app starts and serves endpoints
- [ ] API key auth rejects invalid keys
- [ ] POST /api/drill/plan calls Claude and returns valid JSON session plan
- [ ] POST /api/drill/evaluate calls Claude and returns evaluation result
- [ ] Error handling for Claude API failures (timeout, rate limit, invalid response)
- [ ] Docker build succeeds
- [ ] Flutter ClaudeClient can call both endpoints

jobs.title: Ticket 11: Claude Drill Session Orchestration
Ticket 12: Claude Response Evaluation for Ambiguous Scores
Ticket 13: Cross-Session SRS Scheduling (SM-2)
Ticket 14: Progress Dashboard Screen
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 2 — Intelligence
**Blocked by:** #13
**Blocks:** None

---

## Summary

Build a progress dashboard that shows the user's learning trajectory: items learned, retention rates, daily practice streaks, and breakdown by difficulty level. This motivates continued practice.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Data queries needed

Add to `AppDatabase` or `ProgressRepository`:

```dart
/// Items mastered (repetitionCount >= 5 and lastScore >= 0.8)
Future<int> getMasteredCount()

/// Items in progress (0 < repetitionCount < 5)
Future<int> getInProgressCount()

/// Items not yet seen (repetitionCount == 0)
Future<int> getNotStartedCount()

/// Average score across all items with at least 1 review
Future<double> getAverageScore()

/// Drill sessions per day for the last N days
Future<Map<DateTime, int>> getSessionsPerDay({int days = 30})

/// Total drill time (sum of session durations) for the last N days
Future<Duration> getTotalDrillTime({int days = 30})

/// Current streak (consecutive days with at least 1 session)
Future<int> getCurrentStreak()

/// Items by difficulty with average score
Future<Map<int, ({int count, double avgScore})>> getItemsByDifficulty()
```

### 2. Screen layout

#### `lib/features/progress/progress_screen.dart`

```
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│       Your Progress          │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│                              │
│  ┌──────┐ ┌──────┐ ┌──────┐ │
│  │  12  │ │  28  │ │  10  │ │  ← stat cards
│  │Master│ │Learn │ │ New  │ │
│  └──────┘ └──────┘ └──────┘ │
│                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │  🔥 5 day streak    │   │  ← streak badge
│  │  Total: 2h 30m      │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  Practice History (30 days)  │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │  ▃▅▇█▅▃▇█▃▅▇        │   │  ← simple bar chart
│  │  (sessions per day)  │   │    (no charting lib needed)
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  By Difficulty               │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ ★☆☆ Beginner  85%   │   │  ← difficulty breakdown
│  │ ★★☆ Elementary 72%  │   │
│  │ ★★★ Intermed.  60%  │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

### 3. Stat cards

Three cards at the top:
- **Mastered**: items with 5+ successful reps and last score ≥ 0.8. Green background.
- **Learning**: items with 1-4 reps. Yellow background.
- **New**: items with 0 reps. Gray background.

### 4. Practice history bar chart

Simple bar chart showing sessions per day for the last 30 days. Build with `CustomPainter` — no need for a charting library for this simple visualization.

```dart
class PracticeHistoryChart extends StatelessWidget {
  final Map<DateTime, int> sessionsPerDay;

  // Render vertical bars for each day
  // Height proportional to session count
  // Today highlighted
  // Show date labels for every 7th day
}
```

### 5. Navigation

Add progress screen to GoRouter and add a bottom navigation bar or tab in the home screen:

```dart
GoRoute(
  path: '/progress',
  builder: (context, state) => const ProgressScreen(),
),
```

Add a simple bottom nav to home screen with two tabs: Home and Progress.

### 6. Riverpod providers

```dart
final progressStatsProvider = FutureProvider<ProgressStats>((ref) async {
  final repo = ref.watch(progressRepositoryProvider);
  return ProgressStats(
    mastered: await repo.getMasteredCount(),
    inProgress: await repo.getInProgressCount(),
    notStarted: await repo.getNotStartedCount(),
    averageScore: await repo.getAverageScore(),
    currentStreak: await repo.getCurrentStreak(),
    totalDrillTime: await repo.getTotalDrillTime(),
    sessionsPerDay: await repo.getSessionsPerDay(),
    byDifficulty: await repo.getItemsByDifficulty(),
  );
});
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Stat cards show correct mastered/learning/new counts
- [ ] Streak calculation is correct (consecutive days with sessions)
- [ ] Practice history chart renders for last 30 days
- [ ] Difficulty breakdown shows average score per level
- [ ] Total drill time is calculated correctly
- [ ] Screen is accessible from home via bottom navigation
- [ ] Data refreshes when returning from a drill session

jobs.title: Ticket 15: Backend Ingestion Service — FastAPI + Celery + PostgreSQL
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 3 — Ingestion Pipeline
**Blocked by:** #10
**Blocks:** #16, #17, #18

---

## Summary

Extend the backend (from ticket #10) into a full ingestion service. Add PostgreSQL for persistent storage, Celery for async task processing, and S3 for file storage. This is the infrastructure that powers the tutor session → drill content pipeline.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Extended project structure

```
backend/
├── pyproject.toml
├── alembic/                    # DB migrations
│   ├── alembic.ini
│   ├── env.py
│   └── versions/
├── app/
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── main.py
│   ├── config.py
│   ├── auth.py
│   ├── database.py             # SQLAlchemy setup
│   ├── routers/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── drill.py            # (from ticket #10)
│   │   ├── sessions.py         # NEW: tutor session upload + status
│   │   └── sync.py             # NEW: content sync endpoint
│   ├── services/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── claude_service.py   # (from ticket #10)
│   │   ├── transcription.py    # NEW: Whisper integration
│   │   └── extraction.py       # NEW: Claude content extraction
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── drill.py            # (from ticket #10)
│   │   └── db.py               # NEW: SQLAlchemy ORM models
│   ├── tasks/
│   │   ├── __init__.py
│   │   ├── celery_app.py       # Celery configuration
│   │   ├── transcribe.py       # Whisper transcription task
│   │   └── extract.py          # Claude extraction task
│   └── storage/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       └── s3.py               # S3 upload/download
├── docker-compose.yml
└── Dockerfile
```

### 2. Additional dependencies

```
sqlalchemy[asyncio]
asyncpg               # PostgreSQL async driver
alembic                # DB migrations
celery[redis]          # Task queue
redis                  # Celery broker
boto3                  # AWS S3
python-multipart       # File uploads
```

### 3. PostgreSQL models

#### `app/models/db.py`

```python
from sqlalchemy import Column, String, Integer, Float, Text, DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import ARRAY, UUID
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
import uuid
from datetime import datetime

class User(Base):
    __tablename__ = "users"
    id = Column(UUID, primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    email = Column(String, unique=True, nullable=False)
    api_key = Column(String, nullable=False)  # per-user app API key
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

class TutorSession(Base):
    __tablename__ = "tutor_sessions"
    id = Column(UUID, primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    user_id = Column(UUID, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    session_date = Column(DateTime, nullable=False)
    audio_file_url = Column(String, nullable=True)    # S3 key
    notes_text = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    transcript = Column(Text, nullable=True)           # populated by Whisper
    processing_status = Column(
        Enum("pending", "transcribing", "extracting", "complete", "error",
             name="processing_status"),
        default="pending"
    )
    error_message = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    updated_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow)

    items = relationship("LessonItemDB", back_populates="session")

class LessonItemDB(Base):
    __tablename__ = "lesson_items"
    id = Column(UUID, primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    session_id = Column(UUID, ForeignKey("tutor_sessions.id"), nullable=True)
    user_id = Column(UUID, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    item_type = Column(String, nullable=False)  # vocabulary, phrase, grammar_pattern, dialog_turn
    korean = Column(String, nullable=False)
    romanization = Column(String, nullable=False)
    english = Column(String, nullable=False)
    context_note = Column(Text, default="")
    difficulty = Column(Integer, default=1)
    tags = Column(ARRAY(String), default=[])
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

    session = relationship("TutorSession", back_populates="items")

class DialogScript(Base):
    __tablename__ = "dialog_scripts"
    id = Column(UUID, primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    session_id = Column(UUID, ForeignKey("tutor_sessions.id"), nullable=False)
    user_id = Column(UUID, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
    title = Column(String, nullable=False)
    description = Column(Text, default="")
    difficulty = Column(Integer, default=1)

    turns = relationship("DialogTurn", back_populates="dialog", order_by="DialogTurn.turn_order")

class DialogTurn(Base):
    __tablename__ = "dialog_turns"
    id = Column(UUID, primary_key=True, default=uuid.uuid4)
    dialog_id = Column(UUID, ForeignKey("dialog_scripts.id"), nullable=False)
    turn_order = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    speaker_role = Column(Enum("tutor", "student", name="speaker_role"), nullable=False)
    korean = Column(String, nullable=False)
    english = Column(String, nullable=False)

    dialog = relationship("DialogScript", back_populates="turns")
```

### 4. Database setup

#### `app/database.py`

```python
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import create_async_engine, AsyncSession
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker

engine = create_async_engine(settings.database_url, echo=False)
AsyncSessionLocal = sessionmaker(engine, class_=AsyncSession, expire_on_commit=False)

async def get_db():
    async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session:
        yield session
```

### 5. S3 storage service

#### `app/storage/s3.py`

```python
import boto3

class S3Storage:
    def __init__(self, bucket: str, region: str):
        self.s3 = boto3.client('s3', region_name=region)
        self.bucket = bucket

    def generate_upload_url(self, key: str, expires_in: int = 3600) -> str:
        """Generate a presigned URL for the app to upload directly to S3."""
        return self.s3.generate_presigned_url(
            'put_object',
            Params={'Bucket': self.bucket, 'Key': key},
            ExpiresIn=expires_in,
        )

    def download_file(self, key: str, local_path: str):
        """Download a file from S3 (used by Celery workers)."""
        self.s3.download_file(self.bucket, key, local_path)

    def get_file_url(self, key: str) -> str:
        return f"s3://{self.bucket}/{key}"
```

### 6. Celery configuration

#### `app/tasks/celery_app.py`

```python
from celery import Celery

celery_app = Celery(
    "studybuddy",
    broker=settings.redis_url,
    backend=settings.redis_url,
)

celery_app.conf.update(
    task_serializer="json",
    result_serializer="json",
    accept_content=["json"],
    timezone="UTC",
    task_track_started=True,
    task_acks_late=True,
)
```

### 7. Session upload endpoint

#### `app/routers/sessions.py`

```python
@router.post("/api/sessions/upload")
async def upload_session(
    session_date: str = Form(...),
    notes: str = Form(None),
    audio: UploadFile = File(None),
    user = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Upload a tutor session (audio + notes) for processing."""

    # Create session record
    session = TutorSession(
        user_id=user.id,
        session_date=datetime.fromisoformat(session_date),
        notes_text=notes,
        processing_status="pending",
    )

    # Upload audio to S3 if provided
    if audio:
        s3_key = f"sessions/{user.id}/{session.id}/{audio.filename}"
        # Upload to S3...
        session.audio_file_url = s3_key

    db.add(session)
    await db.commit()

    # Kick off processing pipeline
    from app.tasks.transcribe import transcribe_session
    transcribe_session.delay(str(session.id))

    return {"session_id": str(session.id), "status": "pending"}

@router.get("/api/sessions/{session_id}/status")
async def get_session_status(session_id: str, ...):
    """Check processing status of a session."""
    ...
```

### 8. Sync endpoint

#### `app/routers/sync.py`

```python
@router.get("/api/sync")
async def sync_content(
    since: datetime = Query(...),
    user = Depends(get_current_user),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
):
    """Return all lesson items and dialogs created/updated since timestamp."""
    items = await db.execute(
        select(LessonItemDB)
        .where(LessonItemDB.user_id == user.id)
        .where(LessonItemDB.created_at >= since)
    )
    dialogs = await db.execute(
        select(DialogScript)
        .where(DialogScript.user_id == user.id)
        .where(DialogScript.created_at >= since)
        .options(selectinload(DialogScript.turns))
    )
    return {
        "items": [item_to_dict(i) for i in items.scalars()],
        "dialogs": [dialog_to_dict(d) for d in dialogs.scalars()],
        "synced_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    }
```

### 9. Docker Compose

```yaml
version: '3.8'
services:
  api:
    build: .
    ports: ["8000:8000"]
    env_file: .env
    depends_on: [db, redis]

  worker:
    build: .
    command: celery -A app.tasks.celery_app worker -l info
    env_file: .env
    depends_on: [db, redis]

  db:
    image: postgres:16
    environment:
      POSTGRES_DB: studybuddy
      POSTGRES_USER: studybuddy
      POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${DB_PASSWORD}
    volumes: [pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data]
    ports: ["5432:5432"]

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports: ["6379:6379"]

volumes:
  pgdata:
```

### 10. Configuration additions

```python
class Settings(BaseSettings):
    # ... existing fields from ticket #10 ...
    database_url: str = "postgresql+asyncpg://studybuddy:pass@localhost/studybuddy"
    redis_url: str = "redis://localhost:6379/0"
    s3_bucket: str = "studybuddy-sessions"
    s3_region: str = "us-east-1"
    openai_api_key: str  # for Whisper
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Docker Compose brings up API, worker, PostgreSQL, and Redis
- [ ] Alembic migrations create all tables
- [ ] POST /api/sessions/upload accepts audio + notes and returns session ID
- [ ] Session upload triggers Celery task
- [ ] GET /api/sessions/{id}/status returns processing status
- [ ] GET /api/sync returns items created since timestamp
- [ ] S3 presigned URL generation works
- [ ] Database queries work with async SQLAlchemy

jobs.title: Ticket 16: Whisper Transcription Worker
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 3 — Ingestion Pipeline
**Blocked by:** #15
**Blocks:** #17

---

## Summary

Build a Celery worker that transcribes tutor session audio recordings using OpenAI's Whisper API. The worker downloads audio from S3, sends it to Whisper with Korean language hint, and stores the transcript.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Transcription task

#### `app/tasks/transcribe.py`

```python
import tempfile
import os
from openai import OpenAI
from app.tasks.celery_app import celery_app
from app.database import SessionLocal
from app.models.db import TutorSession
from app.storage.s3 import S3Storage
from app.config import settings

openai_client = OpenAI(api_key=settings.openai_api_key)
s3 = S3Storage(bucket=settings.s3_bucket, region=settings.s3_region)

@celery_app.task(bind=True, max_retries=3, default_retry_delay=60)
def transcribe_session(self, session_id: str):
    """Transcribe a tutor session's audio recording using Whisper."""
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        session = db.query(TutorSession).filter_by(id=session_id).one()

        # Update status
        session.processing_status = "transcribing"
        db.commit()

        if not session.audio_file_url:
            # No audio — skip to extraction (notes only)
            session.processing_status = "extracting"
            db.commit()
            from app.tasks.extract import extract_content
            extract_content.delay(session_id)
            return

        # Download audio from S3
        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=_get_extension(session.audio_file_url), delete=False) as tmp:
            tmp_path = tmp.name
            s3.download_file(session.audio_file_url, tmp_path)

        try:
            # Transcribe with Whisper
            with open(tmp_path, "rb") as audio_file:
                transcript = openai_client.audio.transcriptions.create(
                    model="whisper-1",
                    file=audio_file,
                    language="ko",           # hint: Korean
                    response_format="verbose_json",
                    timestamp_granularities=["segment"],
                )

            # Store transcript
            session.transcript = transcript.text
            session.processing_status = "extracting"
            db.commit()

            # Chain to extraction task
            from app.tasks.extract import extract_content
            extract_content.delay(session_id)

        finally:
            os.unlink(tmp_path)

    except Exception as e:
        session.processing_status = "error"
        session.error_message = str(e)
        db.commit()
        raise self.retry(exc=e)
    finally:
        db.close()


def _get_extension(filename: str) -> str:
    """Get file extension, defaulting to .m4a"""
    ext = os.path.splitext(filename)[1]
    return ext if ext else ".m4a"
```

### 2. Audio format handling

Whisper supports: mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, m4a, wav, webm.

Tutor session recordings will likely be:
- **iPhone voice memos**: .m4a (AAC)
- **Android recorders**: .m4a or .ogg
- **Zoom/Meet recordings**: .mp4
- **Manual recordings**: .wav, .mp3

If the file is `.ogg`, convert to `.mp3` using ffmpeg before sending to Whisper:

```python
import subprocess

def convert_ogg_to_mp3(ogg_path: str) -> str:
    mp3_path = ogg_path.replace('.ogg', '.mp3')
    subprocess.run([
        'ffmpeg', '-i', ogg_path, '-acodec', 'libmp3lame', '-q:a', '2', mp3_path
    ], check=True, capture_output=True)
    return mp3_path
```

Add `ffmpeg` to the Docker image:
```dockerfile
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y ffmpeg
```

### 3. Long audio handling

Whisper API has a 25MB file size limit. Typical tutor sessions are 30-60 minutes.

For files > 25MB:
1. Split audio into ~10 minute chunks using ffmpeg
2. Transcribe each chunk separately
3. Concatenate transcripts with segment markers

```python
def split_audio(file_path: str, chunk_minutes: int = 10) -> list[str]:
    """Split audio file into chunks."""
    chunk_paths = []
    duration = _get_duration(file_path)
    chunk_seconds = chunk_minutes * 60

    for i, start in enumerate(range(0, int(duration), chunk_seconds)):
        chunk_path = f"{file_path}.chunk{i}.mp3"
        subprocess.run([
            'ffmpeg', '-i', file_path,
            '-ss', str(start),
            '-t', str(chunk_seconds),
            '-acodec', 'libmp3lame', '-q:a', '2',
            chunk_path
        ], check=True, capture_output=True)
        chunk_paths.append(chunk_path)

    return chunk_paths

def _get_duration(file_path: str) -> float:
    result = subprocess.run(
        ['ffprobe', '-v', 'quiet', '-show_entries', 'format=duration',
         '-of', 'default=noprint_wrappers=1:nokey=1', file_path],
        capture_output=True, text=True
    )
    return float(result.stdout.strip())
```

### 4. Mixed language handling

Korean tutor sessions often mix Korean and English (tutor explains in English, practices in Korean). Whisper handles this well with `language="ko"` — it will still recognize English words within Korean speech but biases toward Korean interpretation.

For sessions that are primarily in English with some Korean, consider:
- Detect dominant language from first 30 seconds
- If mostly English, use `language="en"` but post-process to identify Korean segments

For MVP, always use `language="ko"` — the tutor sessions should be primarily Korean practice.

### 5. Cost estimation

Whisper API pricing: $0.006 per minute.
- 30-minute session: $0.18
- 60-minute session: $0.36
- 4 sessions/month: ~$1.00

Very affordable.

### 6. Retry and error handling

- Max 3 retries with 60s delay between retries
- Common failures: S3 download timeout, Whisper API rate limit, corrupt audio
- On final failure: set `processing_status = "error"` with error message
- User can see error status via GET /api/sessions/{id}/status and retry

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Worker downloads audio from S3 successfully
- [ ] Whisper transcribes Korean audio accurately
- [ ] Transcript stored in tutor_sessions.transcript
- [ ] Processing status updated correctly through pipeline
- [ ] Long audio files (>25MB) are split and transcribed in chunks
- [ ] .ogg files converted to .mp3 before transcription
- [ ] Errors are retried up to 3 times
- [ ] Final errors stored with error message
- [ ] Extraction task chained after successful transcription
- [ ] Sessions with notes only (no audio) skip straight to extraction

jobs.title: Ticket 17: Claude Content Extraction Worker
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 3 — Ingestion Pipeline
**Blocked by:** #16
**Blocks:** #18, #20, #22

---

## Summary

Build a Celery worker that sends the Whisper transcript + user notes to Claude API and extracts structured lesson content: vocabulary, phrases, grammar patterns, and dialog scripts. This is the bridge between raw tutor session recordings and drillable content.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Extraction task

#### `app/tasks/extract.py`

```python
import json
from app.tasks.celery_app import celery_app
from app.database import SessionLocal
from app.models.db import TutorSession, LessonItemDB, DialogScript, DialogTurn
from app.services.claude_service import ClaudeService
from app.config import settings

claude = ClaudeService(api_key=settings.anthropic_api_key, model=settings.claude_model)

@celery_app.task(bind=True, max_retries=3, default_retry_delay=60)
def extract_content(self, session_id: str):
    """Extract structured lesson content from transcript + notes using Claude."""
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        session = db.query(TutorSession).filter_by(id=session_id).one()

        session.processing_status = "extracting"
        db.commit()

        # Build input for Claude
        transcript = session.transcript or ""
        notes = session.notes_text or ""

        if not transcript and not notes:
            session.processing_status = "error"
            session.error_message = "No transcript or notes to extract from"
            db.commit()
            return

        # Call Claude for extraction
        extracted = claude.extract_lesson_content(
            transcript=transcript,
            notes=notes,
        )

        # Insert vocabulary, phrases, grammar patterns
        for item_data in extracted.get("vocabulary", []) + \
                          extracted.get("phrases", []) + \
                          extracted.get("grammar_patterns", []):
            db_item = LessonItemDB(
                session_id=session.id,
                user_id=session.user_id,
                item_type=item_data.get("type", "vocabulary"),
                korean=item_data["korean"],
                romanization=item_data["romanization"],
                english=item_data["english"],
                context_note=item_data.get("context", ""),
                difficulty=item_data.get("difficulty", 1),
                tags=item_data.get("tags", []),
            )
            db.add(db_item)

        # Insert dialog scripts
        for dialog_data in extracted.get("dialogs", []):
            dialog = DialogScript(
                session_id=session.id,
                user_id=session.user_id,
                title=dialog_data["title"],
                description=dialog_data.get("description", ""),
                difficulty=dialog_data.get("difficulty", 1),
            )
            db.add(dialog)
            db.flush()  # get dialog.id

            for i, turn in enumerate(dialog_data.get("turns", [])):
                db_turn = DialogTurn(
                    dialog_id=dialog.id,
                    turn_order=i,
                    speaker_role=turn["speaker"],
                    korean=turn["korean"],
                    english=turn["english"],
                )
                db.add(db_turn)

        session.processing_status = "complete"
        db.commit()

    except Exception as e:
        db.rollback()
        session.processing_status = "error"
        session.error_message = str(e)
        db.commit()
        raise self.retry(exc=e)
    finally:
        db.close()
```

### 2. Claude extraction prompt

Add to `ClaudeService`:

```python
def extract_lesson_content(self, transcript: str, notes: str) -> dict:
    system_prompt = """You are a Korean language teaching assistant. Extract
structured lesson content from a tutoring session transcript and notes.

Guidelines:
- Extract ONLY items that were actually taught or practiced in the session
- Assign difficulty 1-5 based on grammar complexity and vocabulary frequency:
  1 = basic greetings, single words
  2 = simple sentences, common verbs
  3 = compound sentences, grammar patterns
  4 = complex grammar, nuanced vocabulary
  5 = advanced/idiomatic expressions
- Include romanization for ALL Korean text (Revised Romanization)
- Tag items by topic (food, transport, greeting) and formality (formal, casual)
- If the transcript contains corrections, extract the CORRECT form only
- For grammar patterns, include the pattern name (e.g., "V-고 싶다") and an example
- Extract dialog patterns as multi-turn scripts with speaker roles
- Deduplicate: if the same word/phrase appears multiple times, extract it once
- Set item type: "vocabulary" for single words, "phrase" for multi-word expressions,
  "grammar_pattern" for grammar structures"""

    user_prompt = f"""<transcript>
{transcript}
</transcript>

<notes>
{notes}
</notes>

Extract all learnable items and return JSON in this exact schema:
{{
  "vocabulary": [
    {{
      "type": "vocabulary",
      "korean": "감사합니다",
      "romanization": "gamsahamnida",
      "english": "thank you (formal)",
      "difficulty": 1,
      "tags": ["formal", "greeting"],
      "context": "Used when thanking the waiter"
    }}
  ],
  "phrases": [
    {{
      "type": "phrase",
      "korean": "이거 주세요",
      "romanization": "igeo juseyo",
      "english": "this one please",
      "difficulty": 1,
      "tags": ["restaurant", "ordering"],
      "context": "Pointing at a menu item"
    }}
  ],
  "grammar_patterns": [
    {{
      "type": "grammar_pattern",
      "korean": "먹고 싶어요",
      "romanization": "meokgo sipeoyo",
      "english": "I want to eat (V-고 싶다 pattern)",
      "difficulty": 2,
      "tags": ["desire", "verb_ending"],
      "context": "Expressing desire with -고 싶다"
    }}
  ],
  "dialogs": [
    {{
      "title": "Ordering at a restaurant",
      "description": "Practice ordering food politely",
      "difficulty": 2,
      "turns": [
        {{"speaker": "tutor", "korean": "뭐 드시겠어요?", "english": "What would you like?"}},
        {{"speaker": "student", "korean": "비빔밥 주세요", "english": "Bibimbap please"}}
      ]
    }}
  ]
}}

Return ONLY the JSON, no other text."""

    response = self.client.messages.create(
        model=self.model,
        max_tokens=8192,
        system=system_prompt,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": user_prompt}],
    )

    # Parse JSON from response
    text = response.content[0].text.strip()
    # Handle potential markdown code blocks
    if text.startswith("```"):
        text = text.split("\n", 1)[1].rsplit("```", 1)[0]
    return json.loads(text)
```

### 3. Validation and deduplication

Before inserting into the database, validate extracted items:

```python
def validate_extracted_item(item: dict) -> bool:
    """Validate that an extracted item has required fields."""
    required = ["korean", "romanization", "english"]
    return all(item.get(field) for field in required)

def deduplicate_items(items: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
    """Remove duplicate items based on Korean text."""
    seen = set()
    unique = []
    for item in items:
        korean = item.get("korean", "").strip()
        if korean and korean not in seen:
            seen.add(korean)
            unique.append(item)
    return unique
```

### 4. Handling poor transcripts

If the Whisper transcript is low quality (lots of errors, very short for a long recording), Claude may hallucinate items. Mitigations:

- If transcript is very short relative to audio duration, add a warning to the extraction prompt
- Cross-reference extracted items: if an item's Korean text doesn't appear anywhere in the transcript or notes, flag it
- Cap extracted items at 50 per session (prevent runaway extraction)
- Include the session's `processing_status` as "complete_with_warnings" if quality is suspect

### 5. Cost estimation

Claude Sonnet for extraction:
- Typical transcript: ~2000 tokens input, ~1500 tokens output
- Cost: ~$0.01 per session
- 4 sessions/month: ~$0.04

Combined with Whisper ($1.00/month) and drill evaluation ($0.40/month), total API cost is ~$1.50/month for regular use.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Claude extracts vocabulary, phrases, grammar patterns, and dialogs from transcript + notes
- [ ] Extracted items have correct Korean, romanization, English, difficulty, and tags
- [ ] Dialogs are extracted with correct turn order and speaker roles
- [ ] Items are validated (required fields present) before insertion
- [ ] Duplicate items (same Korean text) are deduplicated
- [ ] Extraction results are stored in PostgreSQL
- [ ] Session status updates to "complete" after successful extraction
- [ ] Errors are retried up to 3 times
- [ ] JSON parsing handles markdown code blocks in Claude response
- [ ] Cap of 50 items per session prevents runaway extraction

jobs.title: Ticket 18: App ↔ Backend Sync
Ticket 19: Upload Screen + Lesson Browser
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 3 — Ingestion Pipeline
**Blocked by:** #18
**Blocks:** None

---

## Summary

Build two new screens: (1) an upload screen where users submit tutor session audio + notes for processing, and (2) a lesson browser that shows extracted content from each tutor session.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Upload screen

#### `lib/features/upload/upload_screen.dart`

```
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│      Upload Session          │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│                              │
│  Session Date                │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ March 25, 2026  📅   │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  Audio Recording             │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ 🎤 Record   📁 Pick  │   │
│  │                      │   │
│  │ tutor_session.m4a    │   │  ← file name after selection
│  │ 32:15 · 4.2 MB      │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  Session Notes               │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ Today we practiced   │   │
│  │ ordering food at a   │   │  ← multiline text field
│  │ restaurant. New      │   │
│  │ vocab: 비빔밥, 냉면...│   │
│  │                      │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │    Upload & Process  │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Functionality:

1. **Date picker**: defaults to today, user can change
2. **Audio selection**: two options
   - **Record**: uses `record` package to record audio directly in-app
   - **Pick file**: uses `file_picker` package to select from device storage
   - Shows file name, duration, and size after selection
3. **Notes text field**: multiline, optional but recommended
4. **Upload flow**:
   - Get presigned S3 URL from backend
   - Upload audio directly to S3 from app (avoids sending through API)
   - POST to `/api/sessions/upload` with S3 key + notes
   - Show processing status indicator
   - Poll `/api/sessions/{id}/status` until complete

#### Additional dependencies:

```bash
flutter pub add file_picker record
```

### 2. Upload state management

```dart
enum UploadStatus { idle, uploading, processing, complete, error }

class UploadState {
  final UploadStatus status;
  final String? audioFilePath;
  final String? audioFileName;
  final String? notes;
  final DateTime sessionDate;
  final double uploadProgress;    // 0.0 - 1.0
  final String? sessionId;       // after upload
  final String? processingStatus; // from backend polling
  final String? errorMessage;
}

class UploadNotifier extends Notifier<UploadState> {
  Future<void> selectAudioFile() async { ... }
  Future<void> startRecording() async { ... }
  Future<void> stopRecording() async { ... }
  Future<void> upload() async {
    // 1. Get presigned URL
    // 2. Upload audio to S3 with progress tracking
    // 3. POST session to backend
    // 4. Start polling for status
  }
}
```

### 3. Processing status polling

After upload, poll the backend every 5 seconds until processing is complete:

```dart
Future<void> _pollProcessingStatus(String sessionId) async {
  state = state.copyWith(status: UploadStatus.processing);

  while (true) {
    await Future.delayed(const Duration(seconds: 5));
    final status = await _syncClient.getSessionStatus(sessionId);

    state = state.copyWith(processingStatus: status.status);

    if (status.status == 'complete') {
      state = state.copyWith(status: UploadStatus.complete);
      // Trigger sync to pull new content
      ref.invalidate(syncOnLaunchProvider);
      break;
    }
    if (status.status == 'error') {
      state = state.copyWith(
        status: UploadStatus.error,
        errorMessage: status.errorMessage,
      );
      break;
    }
  }
}
```

### 4. Lesson browser screen

#### `lib/features/lessons/lesson_list_screen.dart`

```
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│      Your Lessons            │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ March 25, 2026       │   │
│  │ Restaurant vocab     │   │  ← session cards
│  │ 12 items · 2 dialogs │   │
│  │ ✅ Complete          │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ March 20, 2026       │   │
│  │ Direction & transport│   │
│  │ 8 items · 1 dialog   │   │
│  │ ⏳ Processing...     │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

Tap a session → detail screen showing extracted content.

#### `lib/features/lessons/lesson_detail_screen.dart`

```
┌──────────────────────────────┐
│  ← March 25 — Restaurant    │
├──────────────────────────────┤
│                              │
│  Vocabulary (5)              │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ 비빔밥  bibimbap      │   │
│  │ bibimbap              │   │
│  ├──────────────────────┤   │
│  │ 냉면  naengmyeon      │   │
│  │ cold noodles          │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  Phrases (4)                 │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ 이거 주세요           │   │
│  │ this one please       │   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  Dialogs (1)                 │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │ 🗣 Ordering food     │   │
│  │ 4 turns · Difficulty 2│   │
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐   │
│  │  Drill This Lesson   │   │  ← start drill with only this session's items
│  └──────────────────────┘   │
│                              │
└──────────────────────────────┘
```

### 5. Navigation updates

Add routes to GoRouter:

```dart
GoRoute(path: '/upload', builder: (_, __) => const UploadScreen()),
GoRoute(path: '/lessons', builder: (_, __) => const LessonListScreen()),
GoRoute(
  path: '/lessons/:id',
  builder: (_, state) => LessonDetailScreen(sessionId: state.pathParameters['id']!),
),
```

Add "Upload" and "Lessons" to the bottom navigation or home screen actions.

### 6. Data queries

Add to repositories:

```dart
// Get all tutor sessions (from sync data or local tracking)
Future<List<TutorSessionSummary>> getSessions()

// Get items for a specific session
Future<List<LessonItem>> getItemsForSession(String sessionId)

// Get dialogs for a specific session
Future<List<DialogScript>> getDialogsForSession(String sessionId)
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Upload screen allows recording audio or picking a file
- [ ] Notes text field accepts multiline input
- [ ] Audio uploads to S3 via presigned URL with progress indicator
- [ ] Session is created on backend after upload
- [ ] Processing status polls and updates in real-time
- [ ] Lesson browser lists all tutor sessions with item counts
- [ ] Lesson detail shows vocabulary, phrases, grammar patterns, dialogs
- [ ] "Drill This Lesson" starts a drill with only that session's items
- [ ] Processing errors show meaningful error message
- [ ] Completed sessions trigger sync to pull new content

jobs.title: Ticket 20: Multi-Turn Dialog Drill Engine
Ticket 21: Dialog Drill UI
Ticket 22: Dialog Extraction in Content Pipeline
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 4 — Dialog Drills
**Blocked by:** #17
**Blocks:** None

---

## Summary

Refine the Claude content extraction prompt (from ticket #17) to reliably extract dialog scripts from tutor session transcripts. Tutor sessions often involve role-play conversations — these are the most valuable material for dialog drills.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Enhanced extraction prompt for dialogs

Update the Claude system prompt in `extraction.py` to better identify and extract dialogs:

```
Additional dialog extraction guidelines:
- Look for conversational role-play segments in the transcript where the
  tutor and student practice a back-and-forth exchange
- Common indicators: repeated phrases, corrections followed by correct
  repetitions, scenario-setting language ("Let's practice ordering food")
- Each dialog should have:
  - A descriptive title (e.g., "Ordering at a restaurant")
  - A brief description of the scenario
  - 4-8 turns alternating between tutor and student
  - Speaker labels: "tutor" for the native speaker, "student" for the learner
- If the student makes errors during the dialog, extract the CORRECTED
  version as the student's line
- If the same dialog is practiced multiple times, extract the best version
- Create dialogs even from partial conversations — if only 2-3 turns are
  practiced, that's still a valid short dialog
- If the tutor explains a scenario in English before practicing, use that
  as the dialog description
- Each dialog should have a difficulty rating matching the vocabulary/grammar used
```

### 2. Dialog validation

After Claude extraction, validate dialog structure:

```python
def validate_dialog(dialog: dict) -> bool:
    """Validate extracted dialog has correct structure."""
    if not dialog.get("title"):
        return False
    turns = dialog.get("turns", [])
    if len(turns) < 2:
        return False
    # Must have at least one tutor and one student turn
    roles = {t.get("speaker") for t in turns}
    if "tutor" not in roles or "student" not in roles:
        return False
    # All turns must have korean and english
    for turn in turns:
        if not turn.get("korean") or not turn.get("english"):
            return False
    return True

def normalize_dialog(dialog: dict) -> dict:
    """Ensure dialog turns alternate correctly."""
    turns = dialog["turns"]
    # Ensure turn_order is sequential
    for i, turn in enumerate(turns):
        turn["turn_order"] = i
    # Ensure turns start with tutor
    if turns[0].get("speaker") == "student":
        # Prepend a generic tutor opener
        turns.insert(0, {
            "speaker": "tutor",
            "korean": "시작할까요?",
            "english": "Shall we begin?",
            "turn_order": 0,
        })
        # Reindex
        for i, turn in enumerate(turns):
            turn["turn_order"] = i
    return dialog
```

### 3. Test with real transcript patterns

Common tutor session dialog patterns to handle:

**Pattern A — Explicit role-play:**
```
Tutor: OK let's practice ordering food. I'll be the waiter.
Tutor: 어서오세요. 뭐 드시겠어요?
Student: 비빔밥 주세요.
Tutor: Good! 비빔밥 주세요. 음료는요?
Student: 물 주세요.
```

**Pattern B — Correction-based:**
```
Tutor: How do you ask for the check?
Student: 계산 주세요.
Tutor: Close! 계산서 주세요. Try again.
Student: 계산서 주세요.
Tutor: Perfect!
```
→ Extract: tutor line "계산서 주세요" isn't a dialog, but "계산서 주세요" is a drill item.

**Pattern C — Situational practice:**
```
Tutor: Imagine you're in a taxi. Tell the driver where to go.
Student: 강남역에 가 주세요.
Tutor: 네, 알겠습니다. 강남역이요?
Student: 네, 강남역이요.
```

### 4. Dialog deduplication

If Claude extracts overlapping dialogs (e.g., same restaurant scenario practiced twice), deduplicate:

```python
def deduplicate_dialogs(dialogs: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
    """Remove dialogs that are substantially similar."""
    unique = []
    for dialog in dialogs:
        is_duplicate = False
        for existing in unique:
            similarity = _dialog_similarity(dialog, existing)
            if similarity > 0.8:
                # Keep the longer/better version
                if len(dialog.get("turns", [])) > len(existing.get("turns", [])):
                    unique.remove(existing)
                    unique.append(dialog)
                is_duplicate = True
                break
        if not is_duplicate:
            unique.append(dialog)
    return unique

def _dialog_similarity(a: dict, b: dict) -> float:
    """Compare two dialogs by their Korean content overlap."""
    a_korean = set(t["korean"] for t in a.get("turns", []))
    b_korean = set(t["korean"] for t in b.get("turns", []))
    if not a_korean or not b_korean:
        return 0.0
    intersection = a_korean & b_korean
    union = a_korean | b_korean
    return len(intersection) / len(union)
```

### 5. Testing

Create 3-4 sample tutor session transcripts (in the test fixtures) that exercise different dialog patterns. Run extraction and verify:

- Dialogs are correctly identified and extracted
- Turn order is correct
- Speaker roles are assigned correctly
- Corrections result in correct student lines, not the errors
- Dialog titles are descriptive
- Difficulty ratings match content complexity

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Claude extraction prompt reliably identifies dialog segments in transcripts
- [ ] Extracted dialogs have correct turn structure (alternating tutor/student)
- [ ] Student corrections are resolved to correct form
- [ ] Dialog validation catches malformed dialogs
- [ ] Similar dialogs are deduplicated
- [ ] Dialogs from real tutor transcript patterns extract correctly
- [ ] Short dialogs (2-3 turns) are accepted
- [ ] Dialog difficulty ratings match vocabulary/grammar level
